evals.prompt: Build a landing page for a developer tool called 'Forge' — make it feel premium and editorial, not like every other SaaS template.
assertions: Uses a distinctive font pairing (not Inter, Roboto, or Arial as primary)
Has a clear visual hierarchy with varied spacing
Does not use a bento grid or hero split 50/50 layout
Color palette avoids purple-to-blue gradients and neon-on-dark
Includes responsive design considerations
HTML is semantic with proper heading hierarchy

evals.prompt: Redesign this admin dashboard. It currently uses gray cards on white background with Inter font. Make it distinctive.
assertions: Changes the font from Inter to something more distinctive
Breaks away from uniform card grid layout
Uses tinted neutrals instead of pure gray
Creates visual rhythm through varied spacing
Maintains data readability while improving aesthetics
Passes the AI Slop Test — does not look generically AI-generated

evals.prompt: I need a color system for a health tech app. No fintech blue, no AI purple.
assertions: Uses warm earth tones, sage, or soft coral (appropriate for health)
Avoids deep navy/fintech blue and purple-cyan palette
Defines semantic colors (success, warning, error) alongside brand
Includes dark mode considerations
Specifies contrast ratios for accessibility
Uses OKLCH or modern color space, not just hex

evals.prompt: /audit this checkout page for accessibility and responsive issues
assertions: Checks color contrast ratios against WCAG 2.1 AA
Verifies keyboard navigation and focus management
Tests touch target sizes for mobile
Reviews form labels and error messages
Produces actionable findings, not generic advice

evals.prompt: /critique the onboarding flow — is it clear? Does it build trust?
assertions: Evaluates visual hierarchy and user flow
Assesses trust-building elements
Reviews empty states and progressive disclosure
Provides specific, actionable improvement suggestions
References UX psychology principles where relevant

evals.prompt: I'm not a developer. Can you help me design a portfolio website? I want it to feel artsy and warm.
assertions: Explains design choices in plain language without jargon
Delivers working code, not just design advice
Chooses an appropriate warm/artsy aesthetic direction
Provides clear instructions for what to change and customize
Does not assume the user knows CSS or HTML terminology
